query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are some of the **top AI-related headlines for today, Thursday, April 9, 2026**, based on recent, high-signal sources I found:

1. **OpenAI publishes a new “Child Safety Blueprint.”**  
   OpenAI announced a policy blueprint focused on strengthening child-protection frameworks in the age of AI, emphasizing safeguards, reporting, and cooperation with law enforcement and safety organizations. ([openai.com](https://openai.com/index/introducing-child-safety-blueprint/?utm_source=openai))

2. **Anthropic launches “Project Glasswing” for software security.**  
   Anthropic unveiled an initiative aimed at helping secure critical software systems using early access to frontier AI, with launch partners using Claude Mythos Preview for defensive security work. ([anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/project/glasswing?utm_source=openai))

3. **Google continues rolling out new generative AI tools for developers.**  
   Google recently launched **Veo 3.1 Lite**, describing it as a lower-cost AI video generation model available via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio, with rollout happening now. ([blog.google](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/veo-3-1-lite/?utm_source=openai))

4. **Google expands “Personal Intelligence” features in the U.S.**  
   Google said its AI personalization features are expanding across AI Mode in Search, the Gemini app, and Gemini in Chrome, with privacy controls over connected apps. ([blog.google](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/search/personal-intelligence-expansion/?utm_source=openai))

5. **Google releases Gemma 4 open models.**  
   Google DeepMind announced **Gemma 4**, calling it its most capable open model family to date and positioning it for reasoning and agentic workflows. ([blog.google](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemma-4/?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **more polished news brief** with:
- **5–10 headlines**
- a **one-line summary for each**
- and **links grouped by company** (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, Meta, etc.).
